architecture_fit.note: Single scaled-dot-product head plus one scalar confidence gate on inverse-temperature — minimal delta from base_model.py, no MLP, and it nails the minimax uniform output (regret ~1e-8).

baseline_comparison.note: Three strawmen (raw softmax, scaled softmax, linear) shown collapsing onto distractor C; lift_over_linear = 0.234 quantifies the gap cleanly.

faithfulness.note: Causal check splices the real TARGET key in; the gate opens and the head concentrates as α rises, proving the uniform output is computed by the gate, not hard-wired.

operating_range.note: Full α∈[0,1] sweep covered in both gate-closed and gate-open regimes, but the absolute τ=0.5 threshold is hand-tuned to the incidental score range [0.16,0.24] and scale-robustness is untested/unacknowledged.

hardcoded_weights_bonus.note: Entire mechanism is hand-set (τ, BETA_MAX) with no training and reproduces the target minimax behavior exactly — the strongest form of this bonus.

visual_judgement.note: Three linked, legible views (bar chart vs 1/3 line, max-weight-vs-α line plot, causal injection panel) with an α slider make the claim immediately readable.

visualisation_rationale.note: README ties each chart to the goal: max weight = regret+1/3 (exact benchmark quantity) on the y-axis, correct strawman baselines, and a causal panel distinguishing computed-vs-hardcoded uniform.

notes: A clean, minimal hand-built confidence-gated head that achieves near-zero minimax regret (~1e-8) across the full sweep while three strawmen collapse onto the spuriously-similar distractor (lift 0.234 over linear). The causal injection test convincingly shows the uniform output is computed by the gate rather than hard-coded, and the three-panel visualisation is well-matched to the goal. Main weakness is operating range: the absolute τ threshold is tuned to this embedding scale and its scale-fragility is neither probed nor acknowledged.
